excerpt: every soul is attended at the moment of death by al-mubāshir wa-l-bashīr for the righteous, and by munkar wa-nakīr for the wicked
source_location_hint: eschatological doctrine on the reception of souls at death

excerpt: the believer acquires righteous deeds and true knowledge, preparing the luminous form through the refined sensory and rational souls
source_location_hint: doctrine of the soul's perfection and ascent

excerpt: When God raises a believer from the grave, a righteous form shall accompany that believer
source_location_hint: the angelic form accompanying believers in the afterlife

excerpt: after death the sensory and growing souls merge into one essence, the luminous form, remaining in service to the supreme soul
source_location_hint: the union of souls at death and their continuation

excerpt: the vaporous essences travel to the moon through moonbeams, gathering during the white nights and transferring to the sun by degrees
source_location_hint: the celestial circulation of subtle essences

excerpt: the righteous essence completes its journey with the speed of the piercing star and universal lightning, unobstructed when connected to the Imam of Truth
source_location_hint: the velocity of the righteous soul's ascent through the spheres

excerpt: the righteous essence passing through the Sphere of Captivity and the Two Knots reaches the moon's sky, guarded by watchmen against wicked notions
source_location_hint: the celestial barriers and guardianship of the spheres

excerpt: an Imam who is Lord of Divinity has a gathering of one hundred thousand forms in his divinity, and can identify any form he desires, though he may appear through one thousand veils without necessarily doing so
source_authority: Ismaili doctrine of the Imam's omniscience
source_location_hint: the nature of the Imam's knowledge and manifestation
